question: Are caution or warning labels inside the fax important? If not, can I remove them?
content: Yes. The caution and warning labels inside the product are important safety labels. Do not remove or damage any of them. They identify hazards such as moving parts; keep your hands away from those areas when the parts are moving to avoid injury.
ret: "Yes. The caution and warning labels inside the product are important safety labels. Do not remove or damage any of them. They identify hazards such as moving parts; keep your hands away from those areas when the parts are moving to avoid injury.",[]

evidence_refs.summary: The manual says not to remove or damage caution or warning labels inside the product, and warns that moving parts in the illustrated gray shaded areas can cause injury.

notes: No image used because the text directly answers whether the labels may be removed.
